name: dialog
title: Dialog
description: Modal dialog built on Base UI: focus trap, Esc to close, backdrop dismiss, scroll lock, and focus return to the trigger. Composed parts (Dialog, DialogTrigger, DialogContent, DialogTitle, DialogDescription, DialogClose, DialogFooter) with a required title, a 44px Close button, token-driven backdrop translucency, and reduced-motion-safe transitions.
useWhen: Confirming a destructive or irreversible action before it runs
A blocking decision the user must resolve before continuing the task
A short, self-contained form that belongs to the current page (rename, quick edit)
Required legal or policy acknowledgement before proceeding
avoidWhen: Content long or important enough to be its own page — deep links, printing, and back-button behavior all break in a modal
Non-blocking status updates or confirmations (use an alert, or a toast for transient notices)
Multi-step flows or complex forms (use a dedicated page or route)
Anything that opens on page load or interrupts without a user action — modals must be user-initiated
Supplementary help content (use a link, disclosure, or tooltip pattern instead)

keyboard: Enter or Space on the trigger opens the dialog; focus moves to the first tabbable element inside
Tab and Shift+Tab cycle through the dialog's controls only (focus is trapped while open)
Enter or Space on DialogClose or the icon Close button closes the dialog
On close, focus returns to the trigger that opened the dialog
